title: Cloudflare Q1 2026 Earnings: Strong Results Offset by Layoffs and Weak Guidance
text: Cloudflare exceeded first-quarter expectations with adjusted earnings per share of 25 cents, compared to analyst estimates of 23 cents. Revenue reached $639.8 million, representing 34% year-over-year growth and surpassing the projected $622 million. The company narrowed its net loss to $22.9 million from $38.5 million in the prior year. Despite beating quarterly numbers, the company's forward outlook disappointed investors. Cloudflare projected second-quarter revenue between $664-$665 million, falling slightly short of the anticipated $666 million. This guidance miss proved particularly problematic since analysts had expected the company to raise projections following a strong quarter. The company announced plans to eliminate approximately 1,100 positions, representing roughly 20% of its 5,156-person workforce. Management framed the reduction around what it described as an agentic AI-first operating model rather than pure cost-cutting. The restructuring will trigger charges of $140-$150 million, with $105-$110 million in severance and benefit costs. Shares plummeted more than 16% in after-hours trading. CEO Matthew Prince noted that AI adoption internally had grown more than 600% within three months, yet investors appeared unconvinced the efficiency gains justified the dramatic workforce reduction.
